headline: Apollo Global Management (APO) Leaves Russell Growth Indexes, Is It Still Undervalued?
summary: Why Apollo Global Management Dropping From Russell Growth Indexes Matters Apollo Global Management (APO) was recently removed from multiple Russell growth benchmarks, including the Russell Top 200 Growth, Russell 1000 Growth, Russell 3000 Growth, and Russell 3000E Growth indexes. This index removal can affect how some funds hold or trade Apollo Global Management stock, since many passive products track these benchmarks and periodically rebalance around such changes. See our latest analysis...

content.title: Apollo Global Management (APO) Leaves Russell Growth Indexes, Is It Still Undervalued?
content.summary: Why Apollo Global Management Dropping From Russell Growth Indexes Matters Apollo Global Management (APO) was recently removed from multiple Russell growth benchmarks, including the Russell Top 200 Growth, Russell 1000 Growth, Russell 3000 Growth, and Russell 3000E Growth indexes. This index removal can affect how some funds hold or trade Apollo Global Management stock, since many passive products track these benchmarks and periodically rebalance around such changes. See our latest analysis...

headline: The Week in Numbers: SK Hynix IPO heats up, Samsung's profit surge
summary:
STORY: From a massive SK Hynix IPO to Samsung's giant profits...

This is the week in numbers.

:: $26.5 billion

$26.5 billion is how much chipmaker SK Hynix raised in a Wall Street listing on Thursday.

That was according to a U.S. regulatory filing.

A source said demand for the U.S. shares sale was more than seven times oversubscribed.

The South Korean firm is the world's leading supplier of high-bandwidth memory chips - critical features in advanced processors powering AI systems.

George Godber is a UK Fund Manager with Polar Capital: 

"The chip plays have been the absolute epicenter of the AI capex spend. There's a lot of attention there. There's an awful lot of demand. There's a lot of retail interest. But it is volatile." 

:: $7.7 billion

$7.7 billion is how much Apollo Global Management bid for easyJet.

The U.S. investment firm outbid a rival offer from Castlelake for the British budget airline.

EasyJet said it would support Apollo's proposal and withdrew support for Castlelake just days after agreeing in principle to a deal.

The updates sent the carrier's shares soaring during the week.

:: 46%

Close to 46% is how much Fast Retailing's profit rose during the previous quarter.

The Japanese owner of clothing brand Uniqlo said operating profit hit $1.32 billion in the three months through May.

It managed this while dealing with the Iran war's impact on supply chains and logistics.

It also put the firm on its way to an expected fifth straight year of record earnings.

:: 12

Up to 12 is how many submarines Canada could want Germany's TKMS to build for them.

TKMS beat out a competing offer from South Korea's Hanwha Ocean.

Canadian prime minister Mark Carney said Ottawa will now enter talks with the German company.

"TKMS is the best choice for Canadian workers. It will directly create and sustain an ecosystem of well over 100,000 well-paying jobs across Canada."

:: $58 billion

And $58 billion is how much operating profit Samsung flagged for the second quarter. 

The South Korean tech giant expects a 19-fold leap in profits over the quarter from a year earlier, with revenue expected to soar 129%.

It comes as the global AI boom drives soaring demand for chips, sending prices for silicon sky-high.

That’s a boost for Samsung, which is the world’s largest maker of memory chips.

headline: What Apollo Global Management (APO)'s Removal From Russell Growth Indexes Means For Shareholders
summary: In late June 2026, Apollo Global Management, Inc. (NYSE: APO) was removed from several Russell Growth benchmarks, including the Russell 3000 Growth, Russell 1000 Growth, Russell 3000E Growth, and Russell Top 200 Growth indices. This broad index removal matters because it can trigger forced trading by index-tracking funds and prompt active investors to reassess how Apollo fits into growth-focused portfolios. Now we’ll examine how Apollo’s removal from multiple Russell Growth benchmarks may...

headline: Apollo hijacks easyJet takeover with £5.7bn bid, trumping Castlelake
summary: The budget airline easyJet has swung behind a £5.7 billion (€6.6bn) takeover proposal from the US private equity giant Apollo, abandoning its support for a rival American bidder days after agreeing to that offer.View on euronews
